text: Apple's recent financial performance shows signs of pressure, with Q3 revenue declining 1.39% year-over-year to $94.04 billion and net income falling 5.43% to $23.43 billion. The stock has underperformed significantly, down 15% for the year as of July 18, 2025, while the broader market rallied from April and May lows. Over the past 12 months, AAPL returned -5.80% compared to the S&P 500's 13.56% gain, representing a 19.36 percentage point underperformance. However, the stock has shown recent momentum with a 7.43% return over the past 30 days, outpacing the benchmark by 2.38 percentage points.
